qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Regular Expression Denial of Service (ReDoS) in Content-Disposition header parsing
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding identifies the same vulnerable regex (contentDispositionParamRegex) at lib/index.js:88 in @hapi/content that the advisory describes. The advisory reports ReDoS via catastrophic backtracking in Content-Disposition and Content-Type header parsing regexes. The finding correctly targets the Content-Disposition regex in the disposition() function, matching the advisory's root cause: vulnerable regex patterns susceptible to catastrophic backtracking.

selected_finding.title: Regular Expression Denial of Service (ReDoS) in Content-Disposition header parsing

target_advisory_judgement.reason: The finding identifies the advisory-described vulnerable regex (contentDispositionParamRegex at lib/index.js:88 in @hapi/content@6.0.0). PoC execution confirmed catastrophic backtracking: a crafted Content-Disposition header with 20,000 semicolons after an opening quote caused 546.81ms processing time versus 0.01ms baseline, demonstrating denial of service through CPU exhaustion via the exact vulnerable regex the advisory describes.
evidence: contentDispositionParamRegex at lib/index.js:88 is one of the three vulnerable regexes described in the advisory (GHSA-jg4p-7fhp-p32p)
PoC executed with exit code 0, confirming ReDoS in the Content-Disposition parsing path
20000 semicolons: 546.81ms, 10000: 139.15ms, 5000: 35.51ms, 2000: 5.93ms, 1000: 2.22ms — quadratic scaling confirming O(n^2) backtracking
Baseline normal header processing: 0.01ms — over 50,000x slowdown demonstrated
No authentication (PR:N), no user interaction (UI:N), network-reachable (AV:N)

poc_executions.observed_effect: ContentDisposition parsing with malicious header (20,000 semicolons after opening quote) took 546.81ms vs 0.01ms baseline. Quadratic scaling confirmed: 1000→2.22ms, 2000→5.93ms, 5000→35.51ms, 10000→139.15ms, 20000→546.81ms. The vulnerable contentDispositionParamRegex at lib/index.js:88 exhibits catastrophic backtracking causing CPU exhaustion (DoS).
